reasoning: The Consumer Price Index (CPI-U) hovered around 2.5% to 2.9% over the first 9-10 months of 2025. In September 2025, the 12-month increase in the chained CPI was 2.9%, and in August it was 2.7%. The Polymarket market price for this question closed heavily weighted toward 'No' at 0.02 on November 27, 2025. Since the 12-month running inflation rate is well below the target 5.1%, and we are already in December 2025 without a historic one-month hyperinflationary shock observed to close that >2 percentage point gap, the probability of CPI going over 5% in 2025 is vanishingly small.
